step_by_step_thinking:

The prevalence stems not only from potential dangers associated with certain animals but also from developmental stages where exploration and understanding of surroundings play crucial roles. However, it’s important to note that while zoophobia might indeed be high on the list, nyclophobia (fear of darkness) is another very common phobia experienced by children, especially during early years when nighttime routines introduce uncertainty and anxiety.
